pi_items.source: task.json description: 'Create a new Content Type named Support Ticket (machine name: support_ticket)'. This is the required machine name specified in task requirements, not a claim about pre-existing data.
task.json description: 'Create a test ticket titled Defective Battery for customer janesmith'. This is the required node title specified in task requirements.
task.json description: 'Add a Priority field (List text) with values: Low, Normal, High, Critical.' These are the exact allowed values specified in the task requirements.
task.json description: 'Create an Admin View (Page) at /admin/support-tickets that lists these tickets in a Table format'. This is the required path specified in task requirements.

task.json description: 'The user janesmith already exists'. Verified in setup_drupal_commerce.sh which programmatically creates this test user account via drush: $DRUSH user:create janesmith --mail="jane.smith@example.com" --password="Customer123!"

privileged_info_summary: All metadata values are specifications for task execution on a synthetic Drupal Commerce test environment. The task requires creation of a 'support_ticket' content type with fields for 'Related Order' and 'Priority' (with values Low, Normal, High, Critical), a test node titled 'Defective Battery' linked to an order for the test user 'janesmith' (uid created during environment setup), and an admin view at '/admin/support-tickets' with Priority filtering. No real data, credentials, API keys, or personally identifiable information is involved. All data is programmatically created test fixtures within the containerized environment.
